title: Low Resource Multimodal Translation of Nepali Spoken Words into Emotion-Conditioned Sign Language Avatars
abstract: arXiv:2606.26107v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Sign language communication systems, that integrate emotional expression remain underexplored, particularly for low-resource languages. This pilot study presents NEST-V1 (Nepali Emotion and Speech Transformer - Version 1), a proof-of-concept multimodal framework that demonstrates the feasibility of generating emotion-conditioned Nepali Sign Language avatars from spoken input. As a preliminary investigation, we focus on four common Nepali words ("thank you", "hello", "house", "me") across three emotional states (happy, neutral, sad) to validate our core technical approach. Our lightweight architecture employs a shared acoustic encoder for simultaneous Automatic Speech Recognition and emotion classification, achieving 81.1% ASR accuracy and 79.21% emotion recognition accuracy on a dataset of 600 labeled audio samples from 50 speakers. The system demonstrates 37% parameter efficiency compared to separate model architectures while maintain

Title: Low Resource Multimodal Translation of Nepali Spoken Words into Emotion-Conditioned Sign Language Avatars
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26107v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Sign language communication systems, that integrate emotional expression remain underexplored, particularly for low-resource languages. This pilot study presents NEST-V1 (Nepali Emotion and Speech Transformer - Version 1), a proof-of-concept multimodal framework that demonstrates the feasibility of generating emotion-conditioned Nepali Sign Language avatars from spoken input. As a preliminary investigation, we focus on four common Nepali words ("thank you", "hello", "house", "me") across three emotional states (happy, neutral, sad) to validate our core technical approach. Our lightweight architecture employs a shared acoustic encoder for simultaneous Automatic Speech Recognition and emotion classification, achieving 81.1% ASR accuracy and 79.21% emotion recognition accuracy on a dataset of 600 labeled audio samples from 50 speakers. The system demonstrates 37% parameter efficiency compared to separate model architectures while maintain
translated_title: 低资源场景下尼泊尔口语词汇到情感条件手语虚拟人的多模态翻译
translated_abstract: 手语交流系统中融入情感表达的研究仍不充分，尤其对于低资源语言而言。本项初步研究提出了NEST-V1（尼泊尔情感与语音转换器-第一版），这是一个概念验证的多模态框架，展示了从语音输入生成情感条件尼泊尔手语虚拟人的可行性。作为初步探索，我们聚焦于四个常见尼泊尔词汇（“谢谢”、“你好”、“房子”、“我”）在三种情感状态（快乐、中性、悲伤）下的处理，以验证核心技术方法。我们的轻量级架构采用共享声学编码器，同时进行自动语音识别和情感分类，在来自50名说话者的600个标记音频样本数据集上，实现了81.1%的ASR准确率和79.21%的情感识别准确率。与分离的模型架构相比，该系统的参数效率提高了37%，同时保持了可比的性能。
tldr: 本工作提出了一个轻量级多模态框架NEST-V1，通过共享声学编码器同时实现自动语音识别和情感分类，以低资源方式从尼泊尔口语输入生成带有情感条件的手语虚拟人，在参数效率提升37%的同时保持了可比的性能。
en_tdlr: This work proposes NEST-V1, a lightweight multimodal framework that uses a shared acoustic encoder for simultaneous ASR and emotion classification to generate emotion-conditioned Nepali sign language avatars from spoken input in a low-resource setting, achieving 37% parameter efficiency while maintaining comparable performance.
